description: Bring a fresh Linux/Lightsail node up as a cyberware fleet member in one governed run: base deps, Docker, Tailscale (overlay join), and the always-on syscontrol (enable + Restart=always so services survive reboot). Each step is a governed porter recorded to the run-ledger.
rules: Operator-run with sudo — the porters install packages, write systemd units, and enable services (NOT a confined nobody run).
DRY_RUN=1 plans only: every porter prints what it WOULD run and emits JSON without touching the host. The self-test runs this path.
The Tailscale auth key is read from TS_AUTHKEY_FILE (a *_FILE pointer), never a plaintext var and never logged. Use a non-ephemeral key for an always-on node.
Idempotent: a present Docker / Tailscale / enabled service is reported, not reinstalled.
usage: Set record_store + any vars; plan with DRY_RUN=1, then run for real on the node as a sudo-capable user.
limitation: Needs a Debian/Ubuntu host (apt) + systemd + sudo. Real execution cannot run hermetically in CI — the self-test covers the DRY_RUN plan path through the governed channel.
